posts.raw_content:
Since 2023, Washington State has been quietly taking 0.58% of every worker's paycheck for a new mandatory long-term care program called WA Cares.

The pitch: pay in for 10 years, get $36,500 toward care when you're older.

The reality:

• A $300K earner pays 6 times more than a $50K earner. Same $36,500 benefit. No one advertised that.

• After just 3 years of paying in, you can claim the full $36,500 immediately if you have a qualifying care need. Sounds like a reasonable safety net. Until you see what "qualifying" actually means.

• The care needs assessment can be done over the phone. Per official state documents, it “may be based on self-report and could be verified by a health professional.” May. Could. No mandatory doctor exam required.

• What counts as a care need? Needing help with 3 daily activities. And "someone verbally reminding you to take your medication" legally qualifies as one of them.

The tax collection side? Airtight.

The claims side? A phone call. Self-reported. Assessed by a state staffer.

Nobody would defraud this. Right?

Sources: wacaresfund.wa.gov, SB 5291
